title: AMQP 0-9-1 Model, Virtual Hosts, Exchanges, Queues, and Bindings · Inference Brief: Amqp Entity Model
summary: A diagram sends publishers directly to shared queues, confuses exchanges with storage, ignores virtual-host isolation, and cannot explain how routing keys and bindings produce zero, one, or many destinations. Planned artifact: Add and verify the amqp entity model increment in AMQP entity and routing graph. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace producers, connections, channels, exchanges, bindings, queues, consumers, deliveries, and acknowledgements without collapsing their roles.
Compare RabbitMQ queues, streams, databases, job schedulers, and logs against ordering, retention, replay, routing, throughput, and operational constraints.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Amqp Entity Model · Theory Amqp Entity Model
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 9305 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to derive zero, one, or many queue destinations from exchange and binding evidence; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then change one routing segment and prove the exact destination set. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace producers, connections, channels, exchanges, bindings, queues, consumers, deliveries, and acknowledgements without collapsing their roles.
